---
title: "Tutorials"
subtitle: "Step-by-step guides for modeling ML Systems."
---
These tutorials are designed to build intuition for ML systems using the `mlsysim` framework.
They map directly to chapters in the *Machine Learning Systems* textbook—start at the beginning
or jump to any topic.

::: {.tutorial-grid}

::: {.tutorial-card}
[Beginner]{.tutorial-level .level-beginner}

### Hello World: Single-Node Roofline

Learn to lower a model onto hardware and identify the performance bottleneck.
Understand memory-bound vs. compute-bound in 5 minutes.

[Start Tutorial →](hello_world.qmd){.tutorial-arrow}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[Intermediate]{.tutorial-level .level-intermediate}

### Sustainability Lab: Carbon Footprint

Calculate the energy and CO₂ cost of training a frontier LLM across different
geographical grid regions. Quebec vs. Poland—the numbers will surprise you.

[Start Tutorial →](sustainability.qmd){.tutorial-arrow}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[Intermediate]{.tutorial-level .level-intermediate}

### LLM Serving: TTFT, ITL & the Memory Wall

Model the two physical regimes of autoregressive generation: the compute-bound
pre-fill phase and the memory-bound decoding phase. Discover how quantization
and hardware choice affect each phase differently.

[Start Tutorial →](llm_serving.qmd){.tutorial-arrow}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[Advanced]{.tutorial-level .level-advanced}

### Distributed Training: 3D Parallelism

Explore Data, Tensor, and Pipeline parallelism overhead. Model the ring all-reduce
communication cost and pipeline bubble fraction on a 256-GPU H100 cluster.

[Start Tutorial →](distributed.qmd){.tutorial-arrow}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[Beginner]{.tutorial-level .level-beginner}

### Design Space Explorer: Bottleneck Regime Map

Sweep model size × HBM bandwidth to generate a bottleneck heatmap.
See how hardware generations shift the compute/memory regime boundary.

[Start Tutorial →](design_space.qmd){.tutorial-arrow}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[Intermediate]{.tutorial-level .level-intermediate}

### Data Pipeline Bottlenecks

Discover why your GPU sits idle when the CPU can't keep up. Model
the ingestion and transformation walls that starve accelerators.

[Start Tutorial →](data_pipeline.qmd){.tutorial-arrow}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[Intermediate]{.tutorial-level .level-intermediate}

### Chain-of-Thought Economics

Quantify how inference-time reasoning (K steps of CoT) multiplies
your infrastructure bill from $1.2M to $9.1M annually.

[Start Tutorial →](cot_economics.qmd){.tutorial-arrow}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[Advanced]{.tutorial-level .level-advanced}

### Sensitivity Analysis: Where to Invest

Compute partial derivatives to identify the binding constraint. Then
use inverse Roofline synthesis to derive minimum hardware specs from an SLA.

[Start Tutorial →](sensitivity.qmd){.tutorial-arrow}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[Advanced]{.tutorial-level .level-advanced}

### GPU vs. Cerebras: Architecture Comparison

Compare conventional GPU inference to wafer-scale weight streaming.
See how fundamentally different architectures change which wall binds.

[Start Tutorial →](architecture_comparison.qmd){.tutorial-arrow}
:::

::: {.tutorial-card}
[Developer]{.tutorial-level .level-developer}

### Extending MLSys·im: Custom Solvers & Silicon

Learn how to contribute new hardware specifications to the Silicon Zoo or
build your own analytical solvers using the 5-layer architecture.

[Start Tutorial →](extending.qmd){.tutorial-arrow}
:::

:::

---

## Learning Path

If you're new to ML systems modeling, we recommend this sequence:

1. **[Hello World](hello_world.qmd)** — Understand the roofline model and what determines inference speed.
2. **[Design Space Explorer](design_space.qmd)** — Parametric sweeps and regime maps across hardware generations.
3. **[LLM Serving Lab](llm_serving.qmd)** — Model TTFT, ITL, and KV-cache pressure for production LLM serving.
4. **[Data Pipeline](data_pipeline.qmd)** — Ingestion and transformation walls that starve accelerators.
5. **[Chain-of-Thought Economics](cot_economics.qmd)** — Inference-time compute scaling and cost multipliers.
6. **[Sustainability Lab](sustainability.qmd)** — Apply the framework to a real-world carbon analysis.
7. **[Distributed Training](distributed.qmd)** — Scale to hundreds of GPUs and analyze where efficiency is lost.
8. **[Sensitivity Analysis](sensitivity.qmd)** — Binding constraints and inverse Roofline synthesis.
9. **[GPU vs. Cerebras](architecture_comparison.qmd)** — Compare conventional and wafer-scale architectures.
10. *(Developer)* **[Extending MLSys·im](extending.qmd)** — Custom solvers and hardware specifications.

> **Tip:** All tutorials are Jupyter/Quarto compatible. Run them locally after `pip install mlsysim`.
